---
# ============================================================
# PROJECTS PAGE (proj.qmd)
# Showcases your work with embedded PDF previews so visitors
# can read your reports directly on the page.
# ============================================================

title: "Projects"  
---

```{=html}
<!-- ============================================================
  OPTIONAL: Short intro sentence at the top of the page.
  ✏️ Replace or delete this.
  Example: "Here's a selection of projects from my coursework and personal work."
============================================================ -->
```

➡️ Here are a few of my projects from my coursework and personal work.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

```{=html}
<!-- ============================================================
  PROJECT 1
  ✏️ Fill in every placeholder marked with ✏️
  Copy this entire block (up to the next ---) to add more projects.
============================================================ -->
```

## Analyzing Mac Horvath's 2022 Hot Streak

**Course:** N/A\
**Date:** Fall 2025\
**Tags:** `R` `Data Analysis` `Visualization` <!-- Tags: just type whatever labels apply, wrapped in backticks like above -->

In this project, I use R to explore the 2022 UNC trackman data and answer: what prompted Mac Horvath to go from slump to hot streak? I sought to answer this using data visualization, and t-tests. I found Horvath should continue being patient at the plate and work on his swings against fastballs.

```{=html}
<!-- PDF PREVIEW
  - Replace "example_report.pdf" with your actual filename
  - Make sure the PDF is saved in the same folder as this .qmd file
  - width="100%" makes it stretch to fill the page column
  - height="600px" controls how tall the preview box is — increase for longer docs
  - The <p> fallback text shows only if the browser can't render the PDF inline
    (uncommon, but good to have)
-->
```

<iframe src=Horvath2022turnaround.pdf width="100%" height="600px" style="border: 1px solid #ddd; border-radius: 4px;">

<p>Your browser can't preview PDFs. <a href=Horvath2022turnaround.pdf>Click here to download instead.</a></p>

</iframe>

<!-- OPTIONAL: direct download link below the preview -->

[⬇ Download PDF](Horvath2022turnaround.pdf)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

```{=html}
<!-- ============================================================
  PROJECT 2
  ✏️ Same structure as above — fill in your details
============================================================ -->
```

## Analyzing Corner Kicks in Premier League Matches

**Course:** STOR 538\
**Date:** Fall 2025\
**Tags:** `R` `Statistics` `ggplot2` `Data Analysis`

This project analyzes the effectiveness of corner kicks in the Premier League. We sought to identify trends in the data that answered which factors determined the most dangerous type of corner kick. We found that short corners and corners where the attacking team won initial posession were more dangerous than most others. Our findings were presented to the class and professor at the end of the semester. The data used in this project were collected and recorded by each group member.

<iframe src="project_report_corners.pdf" width="100%" height="600px" style="border: 1px solid #ddd; border-radius: 4px;">

<p>Your browser can't preview PDFs. <a href="project_report_corners.pdf">Click here to download instead.</a></p>

</iframe>

[⬇ Download PDF](project_report_corners.pdf)

------------------------------------------------------------------------
